filings.rationale: DeepSeek-V3.2 holds NIS2 in dev because it is a strong open reasoning model that handles structured statutory prose at flat rate, so the day-to-day build burns no metered credit.
filings.text:
**To:** national CSIRT (NIS2)
**From:** Meridian Trust Bank N.V.
**Subject:** NIS2 Mandatory Incident Notification
**Incident Reference:** inc-8842

**1. Notification Timelines**
This notification is submitted within 72 hours of the bank becoming aware of a significant incident, in accordance with Article 23 of the NIS2 Directive. The incident commenced on 16 June 2026 at 02:14 UTC.

**2. Nature and Significance of the Incident**
The incident involves a cyber attack attributed to the LockBit 3.0 ransomware group. The incident is currently assessed as partially contained. The attack has affected the availability of the core banking ledger and the customer KYC store systems.

**3. Impact Assessment**
Preliminary assessment indicates that 48,211 records have been affected. The compromised data categories include customer name, address, and account number.

**4. Measures Taken and Next Steps**
Containment activities are ongoing. The bank's incident response team is actively engaged in forensic analysis, system remediation, and implementing additional security controls to prevent further unauthorized access. A full impact assessment is in progress.

(CLAIMS)

filings.text:
**SEC Breach Notification**  
**Reporting Entity:** Meridian Trust Bank N.V.  
**Incident ID:** inc-8842  
**Notification Date:** [Today's Date]  

**1. Incident Summary**  
On June 16, 2026, at 02:14 UTC, Meridian Trust Bank N.V. detected unauthorized access to its core banking ledger and customer KYC store by the ransomware group LockBit 3.0. The incident remains partially contained.  

**2. Data Compromised**  
Approximately 48,211 records were affected, exposing customer names, addresses, and account numbers. No additional data categories were accessed.  

**3. Response Measures**  
Containment efforts are ongoing. The national CSIRT (NIS2) has been notified per regulatory requirements.  

**4. Regulatory Coordination**  
This filing constitutes the 72-hour notification under applicable SEC rules. Further updates will be provided as the investigation progresses.  

**Contact:** [Designated Point of Contact]  
**Authority Reference:** NIS2 Directive, SEC Reg. S-P

filings.rationale: Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct holds DORA in dev because its long instruction-following is a good fit for DORA's incident-report field skeleton, and it is a third open family on flat rate.
filings.text:
Incident ID: inc-8842

Date of Notification: [Insert Date]

Regulated Entity: Meridian Trust Bank N.V.

Competent Authority: National CSIRT (NIS2)

Incident Start Time: June 16, 2026, at 02:14 UTC

Description of Incident:
On June 16, 2026, at 02:14 UTC, Meridian Trust Bank N.V. experienced a cyber-attack by the ransomware group LockBit 3.0. The attack partially compromised our core banking ledger and customer KYC store. A total of 48,211 records were affected, including personal data such as names, addresses, and account numbers.

Containment Status:
The incident is currently partially contained. Our incident response team is actively working to fully contain and mitigate the impact of the attack.

Next Steps:
We are conducting a thorough investigation to determine the full extent of the breach and to identify any additional vulnerabilities. We will continue to work with law enforcement and cybersecurity experts to ensure a comprehensive response. Affected customers will be notified in accordance with applicable regulations.

Contact Information:
For further information or inquiries, please contact our incident response team at [Insert Contact Information].

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Position]
Meridian Trust Bank N.V.

(CLAIMS)

events.note: SEC Drafter killed AFTER posting its draft, BEFORE marking the fact-record processed. The draft is in the room; the /next cursor has not advanced.
SEC Drafter re-drained /next after the kill; its round-1 draft is already in the room, so the dedup ledger drops the duplicate. Filed exactly once.

signature.caveat: Demo key: the signature mechanism is fully real (one flipped byte makes it INVALID), but this private key ships with the repo, so it proves 'signed by whoever holds this demo key', not HSM/KMS-grade secrecy. Key rotation, a published key directory, and RFC-3161 timestamping are Phase 2.

consistency.verdict: CONSISTENT: all 3 filings report the same value on every one of the 4 load-bearing facts

controls.verdict: 4 of 6 catalogued controls OPERATED and are evidenced in this run; 2 were not exercised by this run's scenario.

controls.title: Segregation of duties on release (two-key gate)
controls.objective: No single human can both withhold and grant a regulated filing's release; two distinct privileged roles must concur before the filing leaves the room.
controls.mechanism: warden/release_gate.py (TwoKeyReleaseGate) + the AWAITING_HUMAN_SIGNOFF -> HUMAN_RELEASED edge in warden/state_machine.py

frameworks.criterion: The entity establishes structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities (segregation of incompatible duties).
Segregation of duties: conflicting duties and areas of responsibility are segregated to reduce unauthorised or unintentional modification.
Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties.

controls.framework_refs: SOC 2 CC1.3; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.3; NIST CSF 2.0 PR.AA-05

evidence.detail: proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.release.signoffs, sealed at the run chain head

controls.exercised_when: Always operates on any run that reaches a release: two distinct human roles (head_of_ir and general_counsel) each record a release_signoff and the Warden admits HUMAN_RELEASED only after both keys are present.

controls.title: Cross-filing contradiction veto (consistency control)
controls.objective: A regulated filing cannot release while two drafters disagree on a load-bearing fact; the Warden blocks the signoff transition on the conflict.
controls.mechanism: warden/diff.py (diff_claims, UTC/alias canonicalization) gating the awaiting_human_signoff transition

frameworks.criterion: Processing integrity: inputs to processing are complete and accurate; the system prevents processing of data that fails validation.
Application security requirements: information processed is validated for accuracy and consistency before it is acted upon.
Detected anomalies and indicators of compromise are analyzed to characterize the event (here, an inconsistency across filings).

controls.framework_refs: SOC 2 PI1.2; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.26; NIST CSF 2.0 DE.AE-02

evidence.detail: not exercised by this run: no protocol_event:diff_blocked, diff event present at packet.diff.blocked_conflicts

controls.exercised_when: Operates only when a cross-filing contradiction is present: the diff emits diff_blocked and the Warden refuses the signoff transition until the fact is reconciled. A clean run (all filings already agree) records the GREEN diff_passed and the veto is NOT-EXERCISED.

controls.title: Exactly-once handoff under live agent kill (idempotency ledger)
controls.objective: A killed-and-restarted agent never double-files; a re-delivered unit of work is acknowledged and dropped, so each filing lands exactly once.

frameworks.criterion: Availability: the entity processes work to completion under failure; recovery procedures prevent loss or duplication of in-flight work.
Redundancy of information processing facilities: processing continues correctly through component failure without loss or duplication.
The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed to restore correct operation after disruption (here, an agent kill).

controls.framework_refs: SOC 2 A1.2; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.14; NIST CSF 2.0 RC.RP-01

evidence.detail: 4 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.chaos.ledger, sealed at the run chain head

controls.exercised_when: The ledger records every unit of work on every run; the DUPLICATE-DROP path (duplicates_dropped > 0) operates only under a chaos kill that forces a re-post. A clean run records ACCEPTED ledger entries with zero drops.

controls.title: Signed, hash-chained provenance (audit-trail integrity)
controls.objective: The complete, ordered run record is tamper-evident and attributable: any edit, reorder, or omission moves the seal and invalidates the signature.
controls.mechanism: warden/chain.py (per-entry hash chain) + warden/signing.py (detached Ed25519 over the bound sha256 + chain head)

frameworks.criterion: The entity evaluates security events and maintains evidence of system activity sufficient to support investigation and accountability.
Logging: event logs recording activities are produced, kept, and protected from tampering and unauthorised access.
The integrity of stored information is protected; integrity-checking mechanisms verify records have not been altered.

controls.framework_refs: SOC 2 CC7.3; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.15; NIST CSF 2.0 PR.DS-06

evidence.detail: 1 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.replay.chain_head, sealed at the run chain head

controls.exercised_when: Always operates: every run seals its append-only JSONL with a per-entry hash chain and a detached Ed25519 signature over the chain head, so the whole ordered run is attestable and tamper-evident.

controls.title: Statutory deadline clocks (timeliness control)
controls.objective: Each regime's reporting deadline is computed deterministically from the statutory trigger and tracked to a met/breached verdict per filing.
controls.mechanism: warden/clocks.py (UTC instants, business-day and holiday-aware arithmetic) driven from the declarative regime catalog

frameworks.criterion: The entity communicates with external parties (regulators) on matters affecting the system, within the timeframes its obligations require.
Contact with authorities: the entity maintains and meets the reporting obligations to relevant authorities within required timeframes.
Incidents are reported consistent with established criteria, including mandated regulatory reporting timeframes.

controls.framework_refs: SOC 2 CC2.3; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.5; NIST CSF 2.0 RS.CO-02

evidence.detail: 4 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.clocks, sealed at the run chain head

controls.exercised_when: Operates on every run: each in-scope regime starts a statutory clock at its trigger event and the clock is stopped at the filing release, with the deadline and met/breached verdict bound into the signed attestation.

controls.title: Reportability / materiality decision gate (decision control)
controls.objective: A regime that does not cross its statutory reporting threshold is driven to a terminal SUPPRESSED state by rule, not silently filed or dropped.
controls.mechanism: warden/reportability.py + warden/materiality gate (deterministic Python gating an LLM-supplied typed verdict; floor/run_floor.py reportability)

frameworks.criterion: The entity identifies and assesses risks (here, the duty-to-notify threshold) as a basis for determining how they are managed.
Privacy and protection of PII: the entity identifies and meets the applicable reporting requirements, deciding which obligations attach.
Incidents are categorized and prioritized; the reporting decision per obligation is made against defined criteria.

controls.framework_refs: SOC 2 CC3.2; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.34; NIST CSF 2.0 RS.MA-03

evidence.detail: not exercised by this run: no reportability, determination_record, clock_stopped event present at packet.reportability.regimes

controls.exercised_when: Operates only on a run that runs the per-regime reportability beat: each regime's threshold is assessed and a below-threshold regime is gated to SUPPRESSED. A core run without that beat is NOT-EXERCISED for this control.

sod.verdict: SEGREGATED: all 4 separation-of-duties invariants hold across the whole run; no identity spanned a conflicting pair of duties

invariants.title: The two release keys are distinct roles and distinct actors per branch
invariants.detail: all 3 released branch(es) collected two distinct keys (general_counsel + head_of_ir, distinct actors)

invariants.title: No single actor both drafted a filing and released it
invariants.detail: the 3 drafter identit(y/ies) and the 2 release-key identit(y/ies) are disjoint; no overlap

invariants.title: The Warden (gatekeeper) never authored a filing it then gated
invariants.detail: the gatekeeper identit(y/ies) ['warden'] ran only gate actions; none appears as a drafter author

invariants.title: The human release roles are distinct from the drafter roles
invariants.detail: drafter role(s) ['drafter'] and release role(s) ['general_counsel', 'head_of_ir'] do not overlap

operability.clock_set: NIS2 early warning (24h), NIS2 full notification (72h), DORA major-incident (72h), SEC 8-K (4 business days)
operability.slo_line: All 3 filings landed with at least 69.23h of statutory margin; 0 breach(es). Replay byte-identical.

declared_dead.note: SEC Drafter missed its heartbeat: no progress for 2 logical drain cycle(s), past the 1-cycle liveness threshold. Declaring it offline; awaiting redelivery and recovery.

recovered.note: SEC Drafter recovered: its work was already recorded, the redelivered duplicate was dropped, no double-file. Exactly-once held across the declared-dead window.

content_elements.label: Nature of the incident
content_elements.instruction: State the material aspects of the NATURE of the cybersecurity incident from the fact-record (what occurred, the threat actor, the systems and data categories involved).
content_elements.body:
**SEC Breach Notification**  
**Reporting Entity:** Meridian Trust Bank N.V.  
**Incident ID:** inc-8842  
**Notification Date:** [Today's Date]  

**1. Incident Summary**  
On June 16, 2026, at 02:14 UTC, Meridian Trust Bank N.V. detected unauthorized access to its core banking ledger and customer KYC store by the ransomware group LockBit 3.0. The incident remains partially contained.  

**2. Data Compromised**  
Approximately 48,211 records were affected, exposing customer names, addresses, and account numbers. No additional data categories were accessed.  

**3. Response Measures**  
Containment efforts are ongoing. The national CSIRT (NIS2) has been notified per regulatory requirements.  

**4. Regulatory Coordination**  
This filing constitutes the 72-hour notification under applicable SEC rules. Further updates will be provided as the investigation progresses.  

**Contact:** [Designated Point of Contact]  
**Authority Reference:** NIS2 Directive, SEC Reg. S-P

(CLAIMS)

  <ix:nonNumeric name="cyd:MaterialCybersecurityIncidentTextBlock" contextRef="C-inc-8842">**SEC Breach Notification**  
**Reporting Entity:** Meridian Trust Bank N.V.  
**Incident ID:** inc-8842  
**Notification Date:** [Today's Date]  

**1. Incident Summary**  
On June 16, 2026, at 02:14 UTC, Meridian Trust Bank N.V. detected unauthorized access to its core banking ledger and customer KYC store by the ransomware group LockBit 3.0. The incident remains partially contained.  

**2. Data Compromised**  
Approximately 48,211 records were affected, exposing customer names, addresses, and account numbers. No additional data categories were accessed.  

**3. Response Measures**  
Containment efforts are ongoing. The national CSIRT (NIS2) has been notified per regulatory requirements.  

**4. Regulatory Coordination**  
This filing constitutes the 72-hour notification under applicable SEC rules. Further updates will be provided as the investigation progresses.  

**Contact:** [Designated Point of Contact]  
**Authority Reference:** NIS2 Directive, SEC Reg. S-P

objects.name: LockBit 3.0
objects.description: The threat actor attributed to incident inc-8842 against Meridian Trust Bank N.V..

objects.name: LockBit 3.0
objects.description: LockBit 3.0 ransomware family, the malware associated with incident inc-8842.

objects.name: Meridian Trust Bank N.V.
objects.description: The regulated entity affected by incident inc-8842.
systems: core banking ledger, customer KYC store; data-subject categories: name, address, account_number

objects.name: Incident inc-8842
objects.description: Regulated breach-reporting incident inc-8842: Meridian Trust Bank N.V. affected by LockBit 3.0, approximately 48,211 records, containment partially_contained, beginning 2026-06-16T02:14:00+00:00.

objects.name: SOD-01: Segregation of duties on release (two-key gate)
objects.description: No single human can both withhold and grant a regulated filing's release; two distinct privileged roles must concur before the filing leaves the room. Control SOD-01 OPERATED in this incident; named-framework references: SOC 2 CC1.3; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.3; NIST CSF 2.0 PR.AA-05. Evidence: 6 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.release.signoffs, sealed at the run chain head.

objects.name: AVL-01: Exactly-once handoff under live agent kill (idempotency ledger)
objects.description: A killed-and-restarted agent never double-files; a re-delivered unit of work is acknowledged and dropped, so each filing lands exactly once. Control AVL-01 OPERATED in this incident; named-framework references: SOC 2 A1.2; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.14; NIST CSF 2.0 RC.RP-01. Evidence: 4 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.chaos.ledger, sealed at the run chain head.

objects.name: INT-01: Signed, hash-chained provenance (audit-trail integrity)
objects.description: The complete, ordered run record is tamper-evident and attributable: any edit, reorder, or omission moves the seal and invalidates the signature. Control INT-01 OPERATED in this incident; named-framework references: SOC 2 CC7.3; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.15; NIST CSF 2.0 PR.DS-06. Evidence: 1 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.replay.chain_head, sealed at the run chain head.

objects.name: TML-01: Statutory deadline clocks (timeliness control)
objects.description: Each regime's reporting deadline is computed deterministically from the statutory trigger and tracked to a met/breached verdict per filing. Control TML-01 OPERATED in this incident; named-framework references: SOC 2 CC2.3; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.5; NIST CSF 2.0 RS.CO-02. Evidence: 4 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.clocks, sealed at the run chain head.

metadata.title: Deadline Room control-evidence assessment results: incident inc-8842

import-ap.remarks: The assessment basis is the Deadline Room control-evidence register (floor/controls.yaml); this document reports its results. No separate OSCAL assessment-plan is modeled.

results.title: Control assessment results for incident inc-8842
results.description: Assessment of the Deadline Room control mechanisms exercised by incident inc-8842. 4 of 6 catalogued controls OPERATED and are evidenced in this run; 2 were not exercised by this run's scenario.

observations.description: Evidence that control SOD-01 (Segregation of duties on release (two-key gate)) operated in this incident run. 6 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.release.signoffs, sealed at the run chain head.

relevant-evidence.description: Run-log event 'release_signoff' evidencing control SOD-01, sealed at chain head 723c6cba6b22fc5b2035074617e16deb9f9c7f77e29731e3d534ee2785cfe5b0 (signer 46e30f5bff8c221d)
Run-log event 'protocol_event:human_released' evidencing control SOD-01, sealed at chain head 723c6cba6b22fc5b2035074617e16deb9f9c7f77e29731e3d534ee2785cfe5b0 (signer 46e30f5bff8c221d)
Run-log event 'protocol_event:signoff_opened' evidencing control SOD-01, sealed at chain head 723c6cba6b22fc5b2035074617e16deb9f9c7f77e29731e3d534ee2785cfe5b0 (signer 46e30f5bff8c221d)

observations.description: Evidence that control AVL-01 (Exactly-once handoff under live agent kill (idempotency ledger)) operated in this incident run. 4 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.chaos.ledger, sealed at the run chain head.

relevant-evidence.description: Run-log event 'ledger' evidencing control AVL-01, sealed at chain head 723c6cba6b22fc5b2035074617e16deb9f9c7f77e29731e3d534ee2785cfe5b0 (signer 46e30f5bff8c221d)
Run-log event 'chaos' evidencing control AVL-01, sealed at chain head 723c6cba6b22fc5b2035074617e16deb9f9c7f77e29731e3d534ee2785cfe5b0 (signer 46e30f5bff8c221d)

observations.description: Evidence that control INT-01 (Signed, hash-chained provenance (audit-trail integrity)) operated in this incident run. 1 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.replay.chain_head, sealed at the run chain head.

relevant-evidence.description: Run-log event 'room' evidencing control INT-01, sealed at chain head 723c6cba6b22fc5b2035074617e16deb9f9c7f77e29731e3d534ee2785cfe5b0 (signer 46e30f5bff8c221d)

observations.description: Evidence that control TML-01 (Statutory deadline clocks (timeliness control)) operated in this incident run. 4 proving run-log event(s) present, read from packet.clocks, sealed at the run chain head.

relevant-evidence.description: Run-log event 'clock_started' evidencing control TML-01, sealed at chain head 723c6cba6b22fc5b2035074617e16deb9f9c7f77e29731e3d534ee2785cfe5b0 (signer 46e30f5bff8c221d)
Run-log event 'clock_stopped' evidencing control TML-01, sealed at chain head 723c6cba6b22fc5b2035074617e16deb9f9c7f77e29731e3d534ee2785cfe5b0 (signer 46e30f5bff8c221d)

findings.title: SOD-01: Segregation of duties on release (two-key gate)
findings.description: No single human can both withhold and grant a regulated filing's release; two distinct privileged roles must concur before the filing leaves the room. Status in this run: OPERATED. Always operates on any run that reaches a release: two distinct human roles (head_of_ir and general_counsel) each record a release_signoff and the Warden admits HUMAN_RELEASED only after both keys are present.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: The entity establishes structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities (segregation of incompatible duties).

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Segregation of duties: conflicting duties and areas of responsibility are segregated to reduce unauthorised or unintentional modification.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties.

findings.title: VAL-01: Cross-filing contradiction veto (consistency control)
findings.description: A regulated filing cannot release while two drafters disagree on a load-bearing fact; the Warden blocks the signoff transition on the conflict. Status in this run: NOT-EXERCISED. Operates only when a cross-filing contradiction is present: the diff emits diff_blocked and the Warden refuses the signoff transition until the fact is reconciled. A clean run (all filings already agree) records the GREEN diff_passed and the veto is NOT-EXERCISED.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Processing integrity: inputs to processing are complete and accurate; the system prevents processing of data that fails validation.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Application security requirements: information processed is validated for accuracy and consistency before it is acted upon.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Detected anomalies and indicators of compromise are analyzed to characterize the event (here, an inconsistency across filings).

findings.title: AVL-01: Exactly-once handoff under live agent kill (idempotency ledger)
findings.description: A killed-and-restarted agent never double-files; a re-delivered unit of work is acknowledged and dropped, so each filing lands exactly once. Status in this run: OPERATED. The ledger records every unit of work on every run; the DUPLICATE-DROP path (duplicates_dropped > 0) operates only under a chaos kill that forces a re-post. A clean run records ACCEPTED ledger entries with zero drops.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Availability: the entity processes work to completion under failure; recovery procedures prevent loss or duplication of in-flight work.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Redundancy of information processing facilities: processing continues correctly through component failure without loss or duplication.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed to restore correct operation after disruption (here, an agent kill).

findings.title: INT-01: Signed, hash-chained provenance (audit-trail integrity)
findings.description: The complete, ordered run record is tamper-evident and attributable: any edit, reorder, or omission moves the seal and invalidates the signature. Status in this run: OPERATED. Always operates: every run seals its append-only JSONL with a per-entry hash chain and a detached Ed25519 signature over the chain head, so the whole ordered run is attestable and tamper-evident.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: The entity evaluates security events and maintains evidence of system activity sufficient to support investigation and accountability.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Logging: event logs recording activities are produced, kept, and protected from tampering and unauthorised access.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: The integrity of stored information is protected; integrity-checking mechanisms verify records have not been altered.

findings.title: TML-01: Statutory deadline clocks (timeliness control)
findings.description: Each regime's reporting deadline is computed deterministically from the statutory trigger and tracked to a met/breached verdict per filing. Status in this run: OPERATED. Operates on every run: each in-scope regime starts a statutory clock at its trigger event and the clock is stopped at the filing release, with the deadline and met/breached verdict bound into the signed attestation.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: The entity communicates with external parties (regulators) on matters affecting the system, within the timeframes its obligations require.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Contact with authorities: the entity maintains and meets the reporting obligations to relevant authorities within required timeframes.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Incidents are reported consistent with established criteria, including mandated regulatory reporting timeframes.

findings.title: DEC-01: Reportability / materiality decision gate (decision control)
findings.description: A regime that does not cross its statutory reporting threshold is driven to a terminal SUPPRESSED state by rule, not silently filed or dropped. Status in this run: NOT-EXERCISED. Operates only on a run that runs the per-regime reportability beat: each regime's threshold is assessed and a below-threshold regime is gated to SUPPRESSED. A core run without that beat is NOT-EXERCISED for this control.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: The entity identifies and assesses risks (here, the duty-to-notify threshold) as a basis for determining how they are managed.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Privacy and protection of PII: the entity identifies and meets the applicable reporting requirements, deciding which obligations attach.

props.name: framework-control
props.remarks: Incidents are categorized and prioritized; the reporting decision per obligation is made against defined criteria.
